name: Empty State + Data Seeding

fields.definition_origin: The 'empty state' design pattern was formalized by Pat Allan / Maximiliano Firtman in mobile-design literature ~2010-2012; codified by Jakob Nielsen / NN/g and Apple HIG (post-iOS7) as a discrete design surface. Tania Conrad's 'Empty States are not Errors' (Smashing Magazine 2014) is the canonical writeup. Notion, Linear, Slack, Asana, and Spotify pioneered template-seeded empty states 2014-2020. Andrew Chen's 'cold start problem' framework (2021 book) generalizes seeding to networked products. The PDF Deep Dive cites the empty-state-as-onboarding-failure-point: bare dashboards post-onboarding are the most under-attended drop-off surface.
fields.core_mechanism: Empty states are decision points: the user has just exited onboarding into the product proper and must decide what to do first. With no data and no scaffolding, the user faces the cold-start problem (Andrew Chen 2021) — they don't know what 'good' looks like in this product. Mechanism stack: (1) cognitive scaffolding (Vygotsky's zone of proximal development, 1978) — seeded examples define the achievable next step; (2) status-quo bias (Samuelson & Zeckhauser 1988) — pre-populated content shifts the default from 'create from scratch' to 'modify what's there', which has lower activation energy; (3) modeling theory (Bandura 1977) — example items teach the user what kind of artifacts belong here; (4) IKEA effect — once user customizes the seeded item, ownership transfers; (5) reduces choice paralysis (Schwartz 2004) by replacing infinite blank-canvas with constrained edit; (6) for social/networked products, seeded content solves cold-start before network effects bootstrap.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Lower anterior cingulate cortex conflict-monitoring load (less choice ambiguity). Hippocampal binding of seeded examples to user's mental model of the app. Reduces blank-page anxiety associated with anterior insula activation. [uncertain on direct empty-state fMRI]
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Notion's 'Getting Started' templates (workspaces seeded with notes, todos, wiki structure) — Notion's growth team has cited template-seeding as a key D1 retention lever in multiple founder interviews 2020-2024; reported D1 retention with templates ~2x vs without (internal data, paraphrased in design talks). (2) Linear's example issues on first project creation (canonical reference in Karri Saarinen's design talks 2022-2024). (3) Spotify's onboarding-derived first playlist — genres tapped during onboarding produce a seeded 'Made for You' playlist before user ever hits search. (4) Slack's #general channel + onboarding-bot welcome message seeds first interaction. (5) Asana's example project seeded with sample tasks (canonical empty-state-as-tutorial). (6) Things 3's empty Today list with single 'Magic Plus' affordance — minimal seeding but explicit affordance. (7) PDF Deep Dive: bare dashboard = critical drop-off point post-onboarding (cite, no specific N). (8) Mobbin teardowns of Linear, Notion, Pitch, Cron, Mem confirm template-seeding as 2020-2025 productivity-app standard. (9) Andrew Chen's 'cold start problem' (2021) lays out the seeding requirement for networked products — Facebook seeded Harvard, Tinder seeded USC sororities, Airbnb seeded Craigslist. (10) Distinct from AI Plan Reveal (item 12): seeded data is STATIC (templates, examples), not LLM-generated.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Activation rate (first meaningful action post-onboarding), time-to-first-action post-onboarding (target <30s), Day-1 retention (the canonical empty-state metric), template-engagement rate (% of users who interact with seeded content), customization rate (% who modify seeded content vs delete-and-start-fresh).
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Notion-cited (founder interviews, paraphrased): template-seeded empty states ~2x D1 retention vs bare empty states. Andrew Chen's cold-start playbook: networked products seeded vs unseeded show order-of-magnitude differences in initial user retention. unverified specific A/B percentages — most cases are paraphrased internal data or single-point case studies.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) Template library: 5-10 starter templates per product, each a complete miniature workflow demonstrating a use case. (2) Seeding logic: at end of onboarding, render template-picker OR auto-seed based on questionnaire answers (item 14 cross-step stitching). (3) For social products: seed with public/featured content + suggested follows (Instagram's first-feed problem solved by 'Suggested for you'). (4) For productivity: example items with realistic data (NOT 'Lorem ipsum task 1') — use named examples that model good usage ('Welcome onboarding doc', 'Q3 product goals'). (5) For media: seeded playlists/feeds derived from onboarding inputs (Spotify tap genres → 'Made for You' playlist). (6) Affordance discoverability: empty state must show ONE primary call-to-create button + seeded examples around it; user can edit seed or create new. (7) Dismissibility: seeded items must be deletable individually; do not pin seed content as undeletable (causes future cleanup friction). (8) Progressive seed-decay: first session shows 5 seed items, by session 5 they fade as user content fills; never mix seed with user content invisibly. (9) Telemetry: tag every seed item with `seeded: true` flag for analytics; track interaction-with-seed vs interaction-with-user-created. (10) Server-driven seed templates allow seasonal/cohort updates without app deploy.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Notion 'Getting Started' workspace: seeded with 'Quick Note', 'Personal Home', 'Reading List', 'Goals' template pages — first launch is never empty. (2) Linear: new project seeded with 4-5 example issues including one demonstrating each issue type (Bug, Feature, Improvement, Task). (3) Spotify: tap 3+ genres in onboarding → 'Made for You' playlist seeded immediately on first home screen. (4) Things 3: empty 'Today' list with persistent 'Magic Plus' button + first-launch tip card. (5) Asana new workspace: example project 'Welcome to Asana' with sample tasks demonstrating sections, custom fields, due dates. (6) Slack: every new workspace has #general and #random pre-created + Slackbot welcomes user with onboarding message. (7) Pitch (presentation tool): new deck seeded with template structure + sample slides. (8) Mem (note-taking): example notes seeded showing tag/link conventions. (9) Cron (calendar): pre-populated keyboard shortcuts overlay first launch. (10) Duolingo: first lesson IS the seed — auto-loaded, mandatory tutorial. (11) Headway: first book summary seeded as 'Your first read' before paywall. (12) Strava: empty feed seeded with 'Suggested athletes to follow' from device contacts/location.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary considerations: smaller viewport demands tighter seed (3-5 items, not 15); thumb-zone primary CTA must dominate; horizontal scroll for seed templates can work but limits visibility. Web equivalent: full template gallery often shown on first landing (Notion's web template-picker is more elaborate than mobile). Mobile must avoid information-dense empty states — pick 1-2 seeded items + 1 explicit affordance, defer template-gallery to a 'See more templates' link.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) translucent material lets seeded cards appear layered without visual clutter — perfect for empty-state-as-soft-preview. M3 Expressive (Android 16+) expressive shape + motion makes seed items feel inviting (not 'placeholder'). Both DLs reward illustration-led empty states; avoid generic line-art empty illustrations (AI-slop tell) — commission category-specific imagery.
fields.category_fit: Universal — every category has an empty-state surface. Strongest fit: productivity/B2B (Notion, Linear, Asana, Slack — template-seeding is table stakes), media (Spotify, YouTube, Netflix — seeded recommendations from onboarding), networked/social (Twitter follow-suggestions, Instagram suggested-for-you), creative tools (Pitch, Mojo, Canva — template gallery). Acceptable fit: wellness/habit (Headspace's first-session, Cal AI's first day's plan are seed-equivalents). Weakness: long-onboarding apps (item 17/18) where AI Plan Reveal is the seeding mechanism (no template needed, plan IS the seed).
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. Authoring 5-10 quality templates is the bulk of work; engineering is straightforward (factory + dismissible flag + seeded:true tag). Allocate copy time — bad seed templates ('Note 1', 'Untitled') are worse than no seed. Cross-step stitching (item 14) integration adds 1-2 days.
fields.tooling_options: In-app: native template factory (no third-party needed). Server-driven: Plotline / Lovi / custom JSON-template registry for seasonal updates. Notion's API for inspirational template imports. Lottie for empty-state illustrations. RevenueCat / Adapty for paywalled premium-template tier (some products gate elite templates).
fields.ai_dependency: none required. Distinct from AI Plan Reveal (item 12) — seeding is STATIC. Optional: small classifier (gpt-4o-mini class) to pick which templates to seed based on questionnaire answers (basically a routing decision, not generative). For LLM-generated seed content, switch to AI Plan Reveal pattern instead — they are different doctrines.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude Design to author the empty state as a structured surface, NOT a placeholder. Prompt fragment: 'Build an EmptyState component that accepts a `seededItems: SeededItem[]` array (5-10 items) and renders them as interactive cards plus a primary `CreateNew` affordance. Each SeededItem has `{ id, title, body, isSeed: true, deletable: true, demonstratesFeature?: string }`. Forbid: generic line-art "empty box" illustrations; "Lorem ipsum" or "Untitled" item titles; non-deletable seed content; horizontal carousel of seed items (use vertical stack on mobile). The empty state must be functionally identical to the populated state visually — same component, just rendered with seed data. Wire up telemetry: emit `seed_item_interacted { id }` on tap, `seed_item_deleted { id }` on dismiss.'
fields.stitch_application: Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the post-onboarding empty state for a [productivity/wellness/media] app. Top: warm welcome microcopy (NOT "You don't have any items yet" — that's apologetic). Middle: 4-5 seeded example cards stacked vertically, each with realistic content (NOT Lorem Ipsum), small "Example" pill in the corner. Each card is tappable to open and editable. Bottom: full-width primary CTA "Create your first [item]". Visual: warm cream background #F4EFE6, ember accent on CTA #E36B2C, Cabinet Grotesk welcome headline 28px, Söhne body 15px. Forbid: blank illustrations, "No items" empty messaging, Lucide "Plus" icon trinity, "Get Started" generic CTA, indigo gradient.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI codegen typically generates an empty state as a generic line-art illustration + 'No [items] yet' message + 'Get started' CTA — the worst possible empty state. Specific failures: (1) Lucide-trinity empty illustration (FileText / Plus / Search), (2) Lorem-Ipsum seed content if any, (3) 'Untitled' / 'Note 1' as placeholder titles, (4) seeded items rendered as visually-distinct 'tutorial' cards rather than first-class content, (5) non-deletable seed pinned at top, (6) no telemetry on seed interaction. Counter-prompt: 'CONSTRAINT: empty state is NOT empty. Seed with 5+ realistic example items styled identically to user-created content. Use named realistic examples ("Q3 launch plan", "Reading list — currently reading") not Lorem Ipsum or numbered placeholders. Each seed item is fully deletable. Tag seed items with `isSeed: true` for analytics. Forbid: "empty state" line-art illustrations, generic "Get started" CTAs, non-realistic placeholder content. The primary affordance is "Create [specific noun]" not "Get Started".'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Bare empty state with apology copy ('No items yet, create one!') — high abandonment. (2) Generic line-art illustration replacing seeded content (AI-slop tell). (3) Lorem Ipsum or 'Note 1 / Note 2' placeholder seed (looks broken). (4) Non-deletable seed pinned at top forever (creates clutter). (5) Seed disconnected from onboarding answers (e.g., user said 'I cook keto' but template is generic recipes). (6) Mixing seed and user content invisibly (user can't tell what they made vs what was seeded). (7) Over-seeding (20+ items overwhelms user; pick 5-10). (8) Stale seed (seasonal template still showing in February that mentions 'New Year resolutions'). (9) Tutorial overlay on top of seeded empty state (combines two patterns redundantly).
fields.regulatory_risk: Low. Empty-state seed content is benign. Edge cases: if seed content is user-generated content from other users (e.g., Tumblr suggested posts), GDPR consent for using the content. If seeded items contain LLM-generated content, FTC § 5 substantiation if the content makes claims. COPPA: under-13 users must not see seed content from adult users (curate kid-safe seeds separately).
fields.platform_constraints_2026: Apple HIG: empty states should be 'helpful and reassuring' — Apple explicitly endorses seeded examples over apologetic empty messages. Apple Guideline 4.2 minimum functionality — apps with NO seeded content and bare empty state may be rejected as 'minimum functionality' violation. Android Material Design: empty states are an explicit pattern category — same guidance. Both: seed content must respect privacy (no real user data unless permission).
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2: seeded items must have accessible labels distinct from user items (VoiceOver should announce 'Example: [title]' for seed). Heading hierarchy must be clear (welcome message → seed list → CTA). Screen-reader users benefit MORE from seeding than sighted users — bare empty states are confusing without visual context. Dynamic Type 200% support: seeded card layouts must reflow gracefully. Avoid color-only distinction for seed vs user content (use a 'Example' text label).
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Seed quality compounds — bad templates undermine the whole product impression. Hire a copywriter for templates. (2) Seasonal/cohort updates require template versioning — old users may see stale templates after months without app launch. (3) Localization: templates must be locale-authored, not machine-translated (recipe templates differ by region; productivity templates differ by work culture). (4) Cold-start for networked products is harder than templates — needs supply-side seeding (suggested follows, public posts) which requires cohort engineering. (5) Template + AI Plan Reveal (item 12) overlap — choose one as the activation moment, not both. (6) Don't AI-slop the seed: 'AI-generated example notes' that read as filler defeats the purpose.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Templates require locale authoring. JP/KR: minimalist seeded content preferred (1-3 examples vs 5-10). LATAM: warmer, family-context examples ('Recetas de mi abuela'). MENA: faith-aligned examples for relevant categories. CN: super-app context tolerates denser seeded grids. Productivity template content (work norms) varies wildly — US 'OKRs' seed is irrelevant in many global markets; localize.
fields.prescribed_action: Seed every empty state with 5-10 realistic, deletable example items styled identically to user-created content. Use named realistic examples (NOT Lorem Ipsum, NOT 'Untitled'). Where the user has answered onboarding questions, derive seed content from their answers (cross-step stitching, item 14). Tag every seed item with `isSeed: true` for telemetry. Forbid: line-art empty illustrations, apologetic 'No items yet' copy, non-deletable seed pinned at top, generic 'Get Started' CTAs.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Questionnaire answers feed seed selection. If user answered 'I cook vegetarian', seed template gallery surfaces vegetarian recipes; if 'I run marathons', seed training plans. Cross-step stitching (item 14) makes the empty state feel personalized without LLM overhead.
fields.aha_relevance: Empty state is the FIRST POST-ONBOARDING SURFACE — for frictionless onboarding (item 19) it IS the activation Aha moment. For long-onboarding apps it's the second Aha after AI Plan Reveal. A seeded empty state lets activation happen on the first action; a bare empty state typically blocks activation entirely (user closes app to think about what to do).
fields.measurement_horizon: Day_0 (first action post-onboarding), Day_1 (activation retention), Day_7 (sustained use)
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (welcoming, helpful seed examples — 'Here's a place to start'), Sage (instructive examples that teach the right way), Magician (the seed mirrors the user's identity inputs — 'this was made for you'), Hero (challenging starter goals seeded). Mismatches: Outlaw archetype apps may want bare/raw empty state to avoid 'corporate' feel; Jester apps need playful/absurd seed examples not generic ones. Always tune seed copy register to archetype.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'Bare Dashboard Drop-off' (PDF antipattern) and 'Cold Start Problem' (Andrew Chen). Pairs against AI-slop generic empty-state illustrations. Anti-pairs with AI Plan Reveal (item 12) only as alternative — both solve the same problem at different points in the funnel.
sources: [Empty States are not Errors — Tania Conrad, Smashing Magazine](https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2017/05/empty-state-design-mobile-apps/)
[Linear's Onboarding Approach — Karri Saarinen design talks](https://saarinen.co/)
[Strategic Architecture and Psychological Frameworks for Mobile Application Onboarding (project PDF)](.agent/Deep%20Dive%20Into%20App%20Onboarding.pdf)

uncertain: specific D1 retention lift figures (paraphrased from founder interviews; no published RCT)
neuroscience_correlate (foundational scaffolding theory exists but not in mobile-empty-state fMRI)
